task.title: Hide cold archive data from default CodeWiki operating context
task.summary: Make CodeWiki default state, graph, agency, and UI context hot-only, with cold archive data available only through explicit restore/archive/audit requests and user-facing UI naming as CodeWiki.

goal.outcome: Default CodeWiki operating context exposes only hot working-set data, while cold archive refs and restore indexes require an explicit archive/restore/audit request; user-facing UI labels say CodeWiki rather than Control Room.
acceptance: Generated graph default views expose hot working-set data but do not include cold archive restore indexes or cold refs unless an explicit archive view is requested.

codewiki_state default/summary output and normal graph context omit cold archive data while preserving hot blockers, active claims, unconsumed handoffs, dirty drift, fail/block validation, validation isolation, and publication blockers.
Control Room/internal web UI user-facing strings are CodeWiki where shown to users; internal source/module names may remain Control Room.
Tests cover that default graph/state views hide restore_index/archive refs and that explicit archive view data remains available in generated graph internals or archive-specific view.
Docs and implementation build evidence show that cold data is not deleted, only hidden from default operating context.
non_goals: Do not delete Git archive refs, builds, validation reports, or roadmap archive records.
Do not remove explicit restore/archive/audit workflows.
Do not rename internal file paths/classes solely to remove Control Room.
Do not hide fail/block validation or active publication blockers.

summary: Implemented hot-only default CodeWiki operating context and user-facing CodeWiki UI naming.

evidence: Added focused hot-context smoke test for graph, codewiki_state, and web graph/state models.
Updated existing build and Control Room smoke tests to align archive view and CodeWiki naming expectations.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Graph archive ledgers moved from default gc view to explicit archive view and cold nodes marked default_hidden.
State graph context now filters to hot default and adds explicit archive include section.
Web graph/state model filters default-hidden/cold nodes and user-facing labels say CodeWiki.
notes: Cold data remains in builds/Git/graph archive view; default operating context no longer exposes it.
Control Room remains internal code/module name only.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added focused hot-context smoke test for graph, codewiki_state, and web graph/state models.
Updated existing build and Control Room smoke tests to align archive view and CodeWiki naming expectations.
code_change_evidence: Graph archive ledgers moved from default gc view to explicit archive view and cold nodes marked default_hidden.
State graph context now filters to hot default and adds explicit archive include section.
Web graph/state model filters default-hidden/cold nodes and user-facing labels say CodeWiki.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Generated graph default views expose hot working-set data but do not include cold archive restore indexes or cold refs unless an explicit archive view is requested.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts now keeps restore_index/git archive data under views.archive and removes archive refs from views.gc default classes; archive_ref and compacted build nodes are marked default_hidden.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_state default/summary output and normal graph context omit cold archive data while preserving hot blockers, active claims, unconsumed handoffs, dirty drift, fail/block validation, validation isolation, and publication blockers.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: state.ts reports graph source graph:hot-default, exposes only gc.classes.hot in normal graph context, and exposes archive data only when include contains archive.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Control Room/internal web UI user-facing strings are CodeWiki where shown to users; internal source/module names may remain Control Room.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: control-room.ts HTML title/header/nav/status and ui command launch messages now use CodeWiki/CodeWiki UI while internal function names remain unchanged.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tests cover that default graph/state views hide restore_index/archive refs and that explicit archive view data remains available in generated graph internals or archive-specific view.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/hot-context.test.mjs asserts default graph/state/UI omission and explicit archive availability; builds smoke updated to expect archive-specific restore_index.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Docs and implementation build evidence show that cold data is not deleted, only hidden from default operating context.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Graph/Roadmap/UI docs updated; this implementation build records archive data as explicit context, not deleted data.

closure_brief.user_intent: Make CodeWiki use hot-only default operating context, with cold/archive data hidden unless explicitly requested, and show CodeWiki rather than Control Room to users.
implemented_changes: Moved archive restore index and Git archive metadata to explicit graph archive view.
Filtered codewiki_state graph context to hot data and added explicit archive include.
Filtered CodeWiki UI graph/state models to hide cold/default-hidden nodes and pass validation history by default.
Changed user-facing browser and launch labels to CodeWiki/CodeWiki UI.
Added hot-context smoke coverage and updated existing build/UI tests.
acceptance_evidence: All TASK-071 acceptance criteria mapped in implementation build acceptance_mapping.
non_goals_preserved: Did not delete Git archive refs/builds/validation reports/roadmap archive records.
Did not remove explicit restore/archive/audit data.
Did not rename internal Control Room files/classes solely for aesthetics.
Did not hide fail/block validation or active blockers.
remaining_risks: Callers that directly read generated graph internals must opt into views.archive for cold refs.

risks: Existing callers expecting graph.views.gc.restore_index must switch to graph.views.archive.restore_index or codewiki_state include:['archive'].

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

Implemented hot-only default CodeWiki operating context and user-facing CodeWiki UI naming.

Checks: npm run typecheck, npm run test:features, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:pack, npm test

Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
